title: Lively Infrastructure
author: Amin

opening_move.description: The paper opens by noting a new genre of social science writing on urban infrastructure as sociotechnical assemblage, but argues it has not yet adequately theorised the sociality of infrastructure in places of absence and improvisation — specifically among the urban poor.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Understanding infrastructure's social life in poor informal settlements reveals forms of political claim-making and commoning not visible through standard political economy or citizenship frameworks.

rivals_engaged: standard political economy approaches to urban poverty
theoretical_positioning.description: The paper works with the new infrastructural turn but extends it by grounding it in ethnographic material from informal settlements, arguing for infrastructure as 'gathering force' and political intermediary.

epistemic_mode.qualitative_methods: Ethnographic fieldwork and case study — three organized land occupations and one established favela in Belo Horizonte, Brazil (fieldwork September 2013), based on site visits and collaboration with local researchers, architects, and activists.
data_sources: Belo Horizonte occupations (Eliana Silva, Rosa Leão, Dandara)
Nossa Senhora de Fátima favela in Aglomerado da Serra
epistemic_mode.notes: Empirical material is constitutive: the argument about infrastructure as sociality and political opening cannot stand without the ethnographic cases. However, the paper ends in a normative register about democratic possibility.

case_type.case_name: Land occupations (Eliana Silva, Rosa Leão, Dandara) and Nossa Senhora de Fátima favela, Belo Horizonte, Brazil

confidence_notes: Epistemic mode is clear: this is normative argument with constitutive qualitative empirical grounding. The normative stake is present but relatively restrained — the paper is more diagnostic and theoretical than prescriptive. The move_sequence is unusual in that case_development is very extended and forms the bulk of the paper, before normative derivation appears at the end.
